case.title: TURPENTINE & ROSIN FACTORS, INC. v. UNITED STATES

opinion.h4: TURPENTINE & ROSIN FACTORS, INC. v. UNITED STATES, (1961)
TURPENTINE & ROSIN FACTORS, INC., v. UNITED STATES. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE SOUTHERN DISTRICT OF GEORGIA. No. 556. Decided February 20, 1961.
opinion.p: E. Way Highsmith and J. H. Highsmith for appellant.
Solicitor General Rankin, Assistant Attorney General Bicks and Richard A. Solomon for the United States.
The motion to affirm is granted and the judgment is affirmed.
opinion.h4: NATIONAL PSYCHOLOGICAL ASS'N v. UNIV. OF NEW YORK, (1961)
opinion.p: NATIONAL PSYCHOLOGICAL ASSOCIATION FOR PSYCHOANALYSIS, INC., ET AL. v. UNIVERSITY OF THE STATE OF NEW YORK. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEALS OF NEW YORK. No. 568. Decided February 20, 1961.
Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Louis J. Lefkowitz, Attorney General of New York, and Samuel A. Hirshowitz and Philip Watson, Assistant Attorneys General, for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
